data.title: Dow Jones Futures down as US markets closed; Uber and 4 others rise, Tesla falls post robotaxi launch.
data.description: The recent surge in the stock market has reached record levels as the Fourth of July holiday approaches. Tesla's stock has declined following the launch of their robotaxi, whereas Uber's stock has experienced a significant increase. Dow Jones Futures are falling as U.S. markets are closed, with Uber, a competitor of Tesla, leading a group of five stocks showing promise in entering buy zones.

data.title: Global Switchgear Market to reach $136.65 billion by 2030 with 5.7% CAGR growth from $103.71 billion in 2025.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida, on July 4, 2025, it was reported that the worldwide Switchgear Market is projected to experience a substantial growth from an estimated value of USD 103.71 billion in 2025 to USD 136.65 billion by the year 2030. This growth represents a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 5.7% during the forecast period. The expansion of the global Switchgear Market is attributed to various factors and is expected to have a significant impact in the coming years.

data.title: Billionaire Ray Dalio warns US national debt per family will jump 85% to $425,000 in next 10 years due to new budget.
data.description: Billionaire investor Ray Dalio warned about the U.S. fiscal trajectory after the passage of the 'One Big, Beautiful Bill Act.' With annual spending of $7 trillion and revenue at $5 trillion, the national debt could reach $425,000 per American family in the next decade. The current debt-to-GDP ratio of 100% may climb to 130%, resulting in interest payments increasing from $1 trillion to $2 trillion annually. Dalio suggested that this could lead to spending cuts, tax hikes, money printing, and devaluation, with interest rates dropping. He mentioned the Ricardian equivalence theory, hinting at future tax hikes due to debt-financed tax cuts. This dilemma could impact bond holders negatively.

data.title: Y4Trade launches trading platform with 200+ fiat withdrawal options in Prague, July 4, 2025.
data.description: On July 4, 2025, in Prague, Y4Trade, a proprietary trading company, unveiled its new trading platform aimed at providing traders with a way to secure funding through a unique web and mobile application. The firm's headquarters in Prague will serve as the hub for this initiative, which seeks to empower traders by facilitating access to capital. The platform is tailored to offer a seamless experience for users seeking financial backing for their trading activities. With a strong commitment to advancing the trading community, Y4Trade aims to revolutionize the way traders approach funding opportunities through its innovative platform.
